meaning: n.令人吃惊的回答,听腻了的消息,怨言,严厉的斥责
examples: I bet Sue gave you an earful when you got home.
He got an earful, especially over the libraries.
He gave me an earful of complaints about his new boss.
Lawmakers home for August are getting an earful from constituents about the still-shaky recovery.
But did you get an earful from other leaders here on the Fed decision?
